4 Quality Information Document for OMI_CLIMATE_SI_BALTIC_volume

Issue 4.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_CLIMATE_SI_BALTIC_volume. 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
This document provides to CMEMS users information on the quality of the daily Sea Ice 
Volume (SIV) index for the Baltic Sea.  
The data are daily SIV values are derived from daily sea ice charts. The ice charts are 
produced by manually drawing polygons of ice, where each polygon has a set of 
attributes, including sea ice concentration and level ice thickness. The original data 
used to produce the charts are satellite images (SAR) as well as in situ observations 
(reports) from ice breakers in the Baltic Sea. SIV is computed as the sum of the area of 
the grid cells that have a SIC value larger than 15% multiplied with corresponding s ea 
ice thickness. 
c) Copernicus Marine products used as input for the computation 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
SEAICE_BAL_PHY_L4_MY_011_01
9 (Satellite observations)

2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Baltic Sea Ice Volume 
The accuracy of the index is directly dependent on the accuracy of the sea ice charts 
(product reference SEAICE_BAL_PHY_L4_MY_011_019) from which it is calculated. The 1 
km grids of the ice concentration and mean ice thickness from the FMI and SMHI ice 
charts were compared. For ice concentration the SMHI and FMI ice chart datasets are 
closer to each other at low and high concentrations, and the standard deviation is 
higher for the intermediate values. There are no ground-truths available for validating a 
sea ice volume index and therefore it is not possible to assign a direct accuracy number 
to this product. However, the ice concentration comparison with the radiometer 
product of University of Bremen has shown good agreement with these products. Based 
on tha t comparison the accuracy of the concentration of FMI ice charts has b

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document evaluates the daily Sea Ice Volume index for the Baltic Sea, detailing its computation method and input data sources.
